--- title: "Knitr Basics" author: "Jane Doe" date: "March 22nd, 2021" toc: true format: html: html-math-method: katex --- ::: {.callout-tip} ## Learn more See the full guide on [Using R with Quarto](https://quarto.org/docs/computations/r.html) and [Knitr Code Cells](https://quarto.org/docs/reference/cells/cells-knitr.html). ::: ## Introduction This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see . When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} summary(cars) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` speed dist Min. : 4.0 Min. : 2.00 1st Qu.:12.0 1st Qu.: 26.00 Median :15.0 Median : 36.00 Mean :15.4 Mean : 42.98 3rd Qu.:19.0 3rd Qu.: 56.00 Max. :25.0 Max. :120.00 ``` ::: ::: ## Plot Output You can also embed plots, for example: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true"} library(ggplot2) dat <- data.frame(cond = rep(c("A", "B"), each=10), xvar = 1:20 + rnorm(20,sd=3), yvar = 1:20 + rnorm(20,sd=3)) ggplot(dat, aes(x=xvar, y=yvar)) + geom_point(shape=1) + geom_smooth() ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](basics-knitr_files/figure-html/pressure-1.png){width=672} ::: ::: Note that the `code-fold: true` parameter was added to the code chunk to hide the code by default (click the "Code" above to see the code). ## Interactive Plots You can also add interactive plots. For example: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} library(dygraphs) dygraph(nhtemp, main = "New Haven Temperatures") %>% dyRangeSelector(dateWindow = c("1920-01-01", "1960-01-01")) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ## LaTeX Math You can also include LaTeX math: $$ P\left(A=2\middle|\frac{A^2}{B}>4\right) $$
